problem: I'm building Layer 2 — Stage 2 — of this system. Layer 1 is information COLLECTION: the wanderers go out and collect precise, deep, high-quality information, and the blender blends all of it to surface NEW information that isn't currently available, or that hasn't been combined for this particular task. So Stage 1 CREATES information. Stage 2 then takes it further: it starts STRUCTURING and assembling a skeleton AROUND that information — fusing the collected pieces and trying to make a concept or a feature out of them. That is the first step toward a concept evolving and taking shape. What I'm trying to design is that Stage 2: how it assembles a skeleton and forms a concept out of Stage 1's raw information.
question: Find the most relevant existing knowledge about autonomous multi-agent pipelines that include a collaborator / auditor / evaluator (orchestrator) layer — and what it reveals about how such a pipeline governs ITSELF. Specifically: how does it decide when to STOP and when to keep going; what TRIGGERS a re-route or a new feature; how does it temporarily TWIST its own framing or cushion, and toward WHICH angle, while it keeps generating; and how does it dynamically ALLOCATE — spawn or DROP — agents (how many, and when) to chase divergent, temporary views of the problem and bring back the information that advances the work?
vision: A step toward an AUTONOMOUS pipeline. Through each checkpoint, the system takes a problem, a vision, a hunch, and a question — where the QUESTION is the main checkpoint to reach. To continue the pipeline, every stage — wanderers, sorter, blender, drift-checker, auditor — contributes, and at each stage they assess whether the cushion itself is good or not, how many blind spots there are, and whether the cushion can be TEMPORARILY TWISTED to solve a certain question or to reach the vision. The autonomous pipeline becomes a DRAFT MAKER for the system — it generates the hypothesis itself, end to end, as a real step toward advancing a concept, with the human judging the finished draft.
hunches: For the current setup I'm thinking about adding an EVALUATOR layer that evaluates each step and handles ALLOCATION — deciding which models should look at the question, whether they should see it from a DIFFERENT ANGLE and WHEN, what TRIGGERS that, how to set up the allocation layer, and when to STOP. That orchestration is what I'm planning now. Honestly this is very big and I don't have any solid hunches at the moment — the exact shape of this evaluator/allocator, its triggers, and its stop conditions is precisely what's unclear to me.
